skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
stem: The number of books read last month by each of 10 students in club X is: 1, 2, 2, 3, 5, 7, 9, 10, 10, 11. The number of books read last month by each of 10 students in club Y is: 5, 5, 6, 6, 6, 6, 6, 6, 7, 7. Both data sets have the same mean. Which of the following statements correctly compares the standard deviation of the number of books read by students in club X to the standard deviation of the number of books read by students in club Y?

answerOptions.content: The standard deviation of the number of books read by students in club X is greater than the standard deviation of the number of books read by students in club Y.
The standard deviation of the number of books read by students in club X is less than the standard deviation of the number of books read by students in club Y.
The standard deviation of the number of books read by students in club X is equal to the standard deviation of the number of books read by students in club Y.
There is not enough information to compare these standard deviations.

rationale: Choice A is correct. Standard deviation measures the spread of data around the mean. The values in club X range from 1 to 11 and are widely scattered, while the values in club Y are tightly clustered between 5 and 7 (most equal to 6). Because both data sets have the same mean but the data in club X are much more spread out, the standard deviation of club X must be greater than the standard deviation of club Y. Choices B, C, and D are incorrect because there is sufficient information from the two lists to conclude that club X has the greater spread.
